Initial release: China job site scraping guide with battle-tested scripts

Comprehensive guide documenting 7 major technical challenges encountered
while scraping Chinese job platforms (Boss直聘, 飞书招聘, 字节跳动, 腾讯 etc.)
and their solutions after 8 rounds of iteration.

Includes:
- SPA rendering with Playwright
- Anti-bot bypass via CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol)
- AppleScript fallback for zero-detection scraping (macOS)
- Feishu recruitment portal specialized extractor
- LLM agent hallucination prevention patterns
- Multi-selector DOM extraction with fallbacks

Add menu link discovery from restaurant homepage

When the homepage doesn't contain menu items, scan the HTML for
links containing "menu" in the href and try scraping those pages.
Handles www/non-www hostname mismatches. Falls back to photo OCR
if menu pages are JavaScript-rendered (e.g., Divi theme).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Vercel timeout: robust error handling + reduce max_tokens

The experience generation was timing out because 8192 max_tokens
could take 80-160s with Sonnet, exceeding the 60s Vercel limit.
Reduced to 4096 (plenty for 8-12 blocks). Also added proper
response.ok checking so Vercel platform errors show the real
message instead of "not valid JSON".

Full-stack Notion clone with React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + Supabase.

Phases implemented:
- Auth (login/register, guards, profiles)
- Workspace + Sidebar (tree nav, favorites, resize)
- Block Editor (TipTap, slash commands, floating toolbar)
- Advanced Blocks (callout, toggle, columns, embed, equations, TOC)

- Share & Permissions (5 levels, publish to web)
- Collaboration (comments, notifications, search Cmd+K)
- Teamspaces (open/closed/private, dark mode, keyboard shortcuts, import/export)
- Optimization (lazy routes, code splitting, React.memo, ErrorBoundary)

88 TypeScript files, 8 SQL migrations, zero `any` usage.

commit.message:
Fix Docker worktree git operations for implement stage (#2094)

* Fix Docker worktree git operations: mount .git dir and rewrite gitdir path

Git worktrees use a .git file pointing to <repo>/.git/worktrees/<name>,
but this host-absolute path doesn't exist inside Docker containers.
The agent couldn't commit, causing "No commits between main and branch"
errors on every implement run.

Fix:
- Mount the main .git directory at /dot-git (rw) in all containers
- Detect worktree cwd and wrap commands with a shell preamble that
  rewrites /workspace/.git to point to /dot-git/worktrees/<name>
- Both create_streaming_process and run_simple use the wrapper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Restore worktree .git file after container exits

The shell wrapper now saves the original .git content and restores it
on exit. Without this, the container permanently overwrites the host
worktree's .git with the container path, breaking git worktree remove.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

CS/CV problem.md 문항 제목 및 문제 설명 수정

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ui: 제휴점검색 버튼 클릭 시 바로 모달 + dim 처리

- 주소입력/제휴점검색 모드 토글 제거 → 검색은 항상 주소, 제휴점은 별도 모달
- 제휴점검색 버튼 클릭 → 즉시 모달 팝업
- 모달 내 검색 시 dim 오버레이 + 진행바 표시

commit.message:
Wire banner points and medals to Google Sheets on admin save

ofs-sheets.js:
- Add MEDALS col (32) to ML map; read comma-separated medal banner
  names from Member Log and use as authoritative medal source
  (replaces auto-computed pts >= 60)
- Refactor bannerMap to store raw points; combine with medalSet
  when building player objects
- Add updateBannerPoints(userId, changedBanners): upserts total
  points per changed banner into "Banners points per user" sheet
- Add updateMedals(userId, medalBanners): updates "Medals" cell
  in Member Log with comma-separated list of awarded banners

OFS_Admin.html (saveModal):
- After banner edits: collect changed banner points and call
  OFSSheets.updateBannerPoints if any changed
- Compare old/new medal lists and call OFSSheets.updateMedals
  if medals changed

commit.message:
Remove railway.toml - let Railway UI manage per-service config

railway.toml applies to ALL services and overrides UI settings,
which breaks multi-service setups (backend + ML service need
different Dockerfiles and start commands).
